# UC-22.11.70 ¬∑ File Integrity Monitoring Alerts on Payment Web Roots

## Description

Surfaces Splunk Universal Forwarder or OSSEC/AIDE change alerts on directories serving checkout static content so unauthorized modification of critical files is detected promptly.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes os and sourcetypes linux:audit, ossec, stash. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Scope FIM to PCI web roots only to reduce noise. (2) Correlate with deploy pipeline user. (3) Page on off-hours changes. (4) Store weekly digest for change-detection evidence (Req 11 overlap). Indexes required: `index=os`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=linux:audit`, `sourcetype=ossec`, `sourcetype=stash`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=os` `sourcetype="ossec"` or `auditd` paths; `sourcetype="stash"` from `monitor://` with `hash` in `change_management`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Scope FIM to PCI web roots only to reduce noise. (2) Correlate with deploy pipeline user. (3) Page on off-hours changes. (4) Store weekly digest for change-detection evidence (Req 11 overlap).

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=os (sourcetype="linux:audit" OR sourcetype="ossec") earliest=-24h
| where match(name,"(?i)/var/www/payment|/opt/checkout/dist)")
| where match(key,"(?i)write|attrib|chmod|unlink|create)")
| stats earliest(_time) as first_change, values(key) as ops by host, name
| sort - first_change
```

Understanding this SPL

**File Integrity Monitoring Alerts on Payment Web Roots (PCI DSS Req 10.5.2, 11.5.1)** ‚Äî Surfaces Splunk Universal Forwarder or OSSEC/AIDE change alerts on directories serving checkout static content so unauthorized modification of critical files is detected promptly.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=os` `sourcetype="ossec"` or `auditd` paths; `sourcetype="stash"` from `monitor://` with `hash` in `change_management`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: os; **sourcetype**: linux:audit, ossec. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=os, sourcetype="linux:audit", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where match(name,"(?i)/var/www/payment|/opt/checkout/dist)")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where match(key,"(?i)write|attrib|chmod|unlink|create)")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host, name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.dest | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**File Integrity Monitoring Alerts on Payment Web Roots (PCI DSS Req 10.5.2, 11.5.1)** ‚Äî Surfaces Splunk Universal Forwarder or OSSEC/AIDE change alerts on directories serving checkout static content so unauthorized modification of critical files is detected promptly.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=os` `sourcetype="ossec"` or `auditd` paths; `sourcetype="stash"` from `monitor://` with `hash` in `change_management`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Change.All_Changes` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (file paths), timeline (first_change), heatmap (host x hour).

## SPL

```spl
index=os (sourcetype="linux:audit" OR sourcetype="ossec") earliest=-24h
| where match(name,"(?i)/var/www/payment|/opt/checkout/dist)")
| where match(key,"(?i)write|attrib|chmod|unlink|create)")
| stats earliest(_time) as first_change, values(key) as ops by host, name
| sort - first_change
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user, All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (file paths), timeline (first_change), heatmap (host x hour).
